codeReview.summary: The repository currently contains the expected modern `xcresulttool get object --legacy` guidance/usages in `.factory/library/user-testing.md` and flow reports, but the reviewed worker handoff points to a commit whose diff is unrelated to this feature scope. Because the feature cannot be traced to the reviewed commit/session, contract adherence fails.

issues.description: The handoff `commitId` (`8c720b6f5e4ec2f960c28820c68d9a70b05fefad`) does not implement this feature; `git show` for that commit changes `DashboardQuickSwitchView` and dashboard UI tests only, with no edits to user-testing guidance/scripts or xcresulttool invocation sites required by this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature uses `switcher-integration-worker`, but transcript/handoff work was a no-op verification task. The skill procedure is implementation-heavy (write failing integration tests, implement cross-surface behavior) and does not match misc-infra guidance/script-standardization reviews, which increases likelihood of `followedProcedure` being marked true without meaningful procedural alignment.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md requires integration implementation/test workflow; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId `644dd423-a796-4b48-8f44-c1ba8bc5c5aa` shows grep/manual checks and no scoped xcode integration implementation loop.

sharedStateObservations.observation: When a feature is considered already implemented, handoffs need explicit implementation-commit traceability to the files/lines satisfying expected behavior; otherwise scrutiny cannot map feature completion to an auditable diff.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit diff for feature `misc-infra-standardize-xcresulttool-modern-invocation`. Expected modern invocation usage exists in repository, but reviewed commit/session is not the implementing change and lacks traceable linkage to implementation commits, so this review fails on contract/traceability.
